body:
The `Chen2022` parameterization for terminal velocity depends on ice particles density. We used to assume that this density is constant and the same for cloud ice and snow. As a result we could precompute more coefficients of `Chen2022` parameterization in `parameters/TerminalVelocity.jl` .

Now we want to:
- use different apparent snow and cloud ice densities in the 1M scheme,
- use the predicted particle density in the P3 scheme.

In order to do that we had to move the computation of `Chen2022` coefficients from `parameters/TerminalVelocity.jl` to `Common.jl`. 

This PR is just refactoring and does not change the actual density values. As a result it does not require any changes in our unit tests. I will update those in a separate PR
title: Revamp Chen2022 parameterization to allow variable apparent ice/snow density

body: This finishes up changing 1M scheme o use apparent densities
title: Update 1M scheme to use apparent densities for snow and cloud ice
